results.title: ActEU for Moldova - Activating Communities for EU Integration
results.proposal_summary: People In Need (PIN) Moldova, with EU funding, aims to strengthen civil society organizations (CSOs) and Local Action Groups (LAGs) in Moldova to encourage active participation of local communities and citizens in Moldova's EU integration process. The call supports 10 selected CSOs to identify strengths and gaps through PIN organizational capacity assessment tool (OCAT) and develop a Strategic Development Action Plan (SDAP). Selected CSOs will benefit from tailored training, mentoring, and access to two distinct grant schemes: (1) support for organizational development and institutional strengthening (up to EUR 5,000) and (2) support for CSO-led sustainable local development initiatives (EUR 20,000 to 31,000 EUR). Eligible applicants must be legally registered non-profit entities in Moldova. The deadline for submission is April 1, 2026. Cofinancing is not required.
results.ilo_match_evidence: This call aligns with the ILO's mandate through its focus on strengthening civil society organizations, promoting social dialogue and tripartism, and supporting sustainable enterprises in Moldova which may advance decent work in supply chains.

results.proposal_summary: The Africa Disaster Risk Financing Programme (ADRiFi), initiated by the African Development Bank (AfDB), aims to enhance resilience and response to climate shocks in African countries by improving the management of climate disaster risks. The ADRiFi Multi-Donor Trust Fund (MDTF) provides financing for Climate Disaster Risk Finance and Insurance (CDRFI) capacity development and premium support to vulnerable countries in Africa. All African regional member countries are eligible to access the MDTF. The fund has received close to USD 100 million. There is no deadline specified in the document. results.title: Call for concept notes – Health, education and women's and girls’ rights and empowerment in Afghanistan
eligible_activities: Demand-driven skills training for women and girls (literacy, numeracy, digital, life, business, technical, and vocational skills).

Inclusive services to protect and advance the rights and empowerment of all Afghans, with a focus on gender, ethnic and religious minorities, underserviced populations including those impacted by disability, and support to women-led groups.

Projects must demonstrate a practical approach to operating in the current Afghanistan context while applying creative and innovative ways of overcoming the challenges for Afghans to access the targeted services, especially women and girls in all their diversity.
funding_restrictions: Projects must avoid legitimizing the Taliban de facto authorities.
Funding must not directly support de facto ministries or their institutions.
No construction or improvements to facilities controlled by the Taliban de facto authorities will be permitted.
results.proposal_summary: Global Affairs Canada (GAC) is funding projects in Afghanistan to improve the wellbeing of Afghans, especially women and girls, through increased access to basic needs services in health, education, and rights and empowerment. The funding totals up to CAD$35,000,000 over 4 years with individual projects ranging from CAD$2,000,000 to CAD$10,000,000. Eligible organizations include legally incorporated entities, excluding sovereign entities and multilateral institutions, registered in the Partners@International portal. The deadline to submit a concept note was April 16, 2025. Co-financing of at least 5% of the total eligible direct project costs is required.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The CfP addresses skills development, equality, and social protection, which align with ILO's mandate; however, the restrictions related to working with the de facto authorities could limit ILO involvement.

results.title: Skills development and Technical and vocational education and training fund – Call for proposals
eligible_activities: Developing and managing a fund in line with expected outcomes.

Ensuring fulfillment of GAC’s fiduciary and programming requirements, including a Results Framework (Logic Model, Theory of Change, Performance Management Framework) as well as key risks analysis and associated risk mitigation measures.

Developing a leveraging component that can mobilize additional financial and in-kind support from and in partnership with diverse partners (e.g. other bilateral donors, philanthropic organizations, the private sector, etc.).

Ensuring the focus themes/sectors/target groups are needs-based and relevant to local markets, government priorities, and that the fund’s sub-grants complement existing youth employment and skills development/TVET programming.
Enhancing the evidence/knowledge base to support effective skills development/TVET leading to decent work for marginalized youth.
Managing and reporting on sub-grants to Canadian, international and local organizations, as per GAC’s requirements.
Conceptualization and oversight of the fund’s governance structure, including roles and responsibilities.
funding_restrictions: The manager/s of the fund will not be eligible to receive funding from the fund to implement projects.
results.proposal_summary: Global Affairs Canada is offering a call for proposals to establish and manage a skills development and TVET fund. The fund will provide sub-grants to support skills development and improve access to decent work for the hardest to reach young people, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa. The total funding available is up to $49.5 million over 5 years. Eligible organizations must be Canadian organizations. The deadline for submitting a proposal was March 6, 2025. Cost-sharing is required, with the signatory organization(s) providing at least 5% of the total eligible direct project costs.

theme: Fundamental Principles and Rights at Work
Decent Work in Crisis and Post-Crisis Situations
eligible_activities: emergency financial assistance to those facing or who have recently experienced persecution on the basis of religion, including assistance to address threats of violence, medical needs resulting from violent assault, legal representation, and replacement of equipment damaged or confiscated as a result of harassment for those working to defend religious freedom.
funding_restrictions: None of the funds awarded resulting from this Notice of Funding Opportunity may be made available for subawards, direct financial support, or otherwise used to provide any payment or transfer to United Nations Relief and Works Agency (UNRWA).

None of the funds awarded under this agreement may be used for any initiatives or programs, or any activities that do not comply with Executive Order 14173 titled Ending Illegal Discrimination and Restoring Merit-Based Opportunity.

Recipients of funding under this Notice of Funding Opportunity (including subawards and subcontracts issued by the recipient) will be prohibited from:

(1) delivering any FASC-prohibited unmanned aircraft system, which includes unmanned aircraft (i.e., drones) and associated elements;

(2) Operating a FASC-prohibited unmanned aircraft system in the performance of the award; and

(3) Using Federal funds for the purchase or operation of a FASC-prohibited unmanned aircraft system .

Eligible applicants include U.S.-based and foreign-based non-profit organizations/NGOs, higher education institutions, for-profit organizations or businesses, and Public International Organizations. results.classification_summary: This is a Request for Statements of Interest (RSOI) from the US Department of State, Bureau of Democracy, Human Rights, and Labor (DRL) for programs that support Internet Freedom.  The RSOI is the first step in a two-part process. Upon merit review of eligible SOIs, DRL will invite selected applicants to expand upon their program idea(s) through the submission of a full proposal application.  Eligible applicants include US- or foreign-based non-profit/non-governmental organizations (NGOs), or a public international organization; or a private, public, or state institution of higher education; or a for-profit organization or business.  The deadline for SOIs is January 9, 2025.  ILO is not an eligible applicant type and is therefore excluded.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The solicitation specifies eligible applicants as U.S.- or foreign-based non-profit/non-governmental organization (NGO), or a public international organization; or a private, public, or state institution of higher education; or a for-profit organization or business. The ILO is an International Organization and is not explicitly listed as eligible.

Contain activities that aim to combat mis/disinformation.

Contain activities which look to combat online harassment, threats, or abuse by encouraging platforms to more effectively moderate discussion in online spaces or work to seek out, identify, and report violations on behalf of affected individuals.
Contain offensive cybersecurity efforts, such as hacking, or counter-attacking.

Propose to operate in places where the implementer and their partners do not have sufficient contextual knowledge to operate effectively or safely, or well-established networks which will allow them to build this knowledge quickly.
results.proposal_summary: The US Department of State, Bureau of Democracy, Human Rights, and Labor (DRL) announces a Request for Statements of Interest (RSOI) for internet freedom programs.  The goal is to protect the open, interoperable, secure, and reliable Internet by promoting human rights. Programs should focus on directly circumventing or defeating Internet censorship; preventing and remedying online attacks; advocating against repressive Internet laws and regulations; and researching and documenting key threats to Internet Freedom.  The funding ranges from $500,000 to $3,000,000. The SOI submission deadline is January 9, 2025.  Eligible applicants include U.S.- or foreign-based non-profit/non-governmental organizations (NGOs), a public international organization, a private, public, or state institution of higher education, or a for-profit organization or business. The project aims to build climate-resilient landscapes and livelihoods. The deadline for submission is March 22, 2026. This call is for construction companies and related entities, and ILO is not an eligible applicant.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The call relates to water supply and irrigation schemes, which can contribute to livelihoods, aligning loosely with ILO's focus on decent work, but the call's primary focus is construction which is outside core ILO mandate.

results.exclusion_reason: The call appears to be for the procurement of construction services, indicated by terms such as "Invitation for Bids" and "construction and completion of water supply and irrigation schemes." This resembles a tendering process rather than a grant opportunity.

results.classification_summary: This is an Invitation for Bids (IFB) from the Asian Development Bank (ADB) for the Developing Resilient Environments and Advancing Municipal Services in Punjab Project in Pakistan. results.proposal_summary: The Adaptation Fund offers funding for climate adaptation projects and programmes to accredited National, Regional, and Multilateral Implementing Entities on a rolling basis. These include project funding, innovation grants, readiness grants, learning grants, and project scale-up grants. ILO, not being an Implementing Entity, is ineligible. There are no deadlines since this fund uses a rolling admission process.
